LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS, LP, VCA ANTECH AND ALAN STEWART'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND PAIN AND SUFFERING ALLEGATIONS AND WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS REGARDING EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND PAIN AND SUFFERING FAIL BECAUSE SHE DOES NOT ALLEGE ANY FACTS SHOWING THAT SHE EXPERIENCED A PHYSICAL INJURY NOR IS IT LIKELY THAT THIS DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AN AMENDMENT. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE: (1) ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING PUNITIVE DAMAGES PER CIVIL CODE 3294 AND 3340 AND (2) A COMMON LAW, STATUTORY, OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR FEES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANTS VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALIST, L.P, VCA ANTECH AND ALAN STEWARD, DVM'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-09-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS, LP, VCA ANTECH ALAN STEWART'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO (1) ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT THE EXISTENCE OF A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP; (2) AMEND THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION AND RE-PLEAD THE ALLEGATIONS OF VETERINARY NEGLIGENCE IN THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VETERINARY MALPRACTICE; AND (3) RE-PLEAD THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LACK OF INFORMED CONSENT IN THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Vincent W. Marsella, Esq., from Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith LLP, 415-262-8599 for defendants and moving party
Christine E. Lopez, Plaintiff and opposing party in Pro Per, 415-722-2168

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS, LP, VCA ANTECH ALAN STEWART'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second, third and fourth causes of action. Plaintiff has leave to (1) allege facts to support the existence of a fiduciary relationship; (2) amend the third cause of action to plead a claim for negligent misrepresentation and re-plead the allegations of veterinary negligence in the first cause of action for veterinary malpractice; and (3) re-plead the allegations in the fourth cause of action for lack of informed consent in the first cause of action.
Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Vincent W. Marsella, Esq., from Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard & Smith LLP, 415-262-8599 for defendants and moving party
Christine E. Lopez, Plaintiff and opposing party in Pro Per, 415-722-2168

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS, LP, VCA ANTECH AND ALAN STEWART'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted without leave to amend as to the emotional distress and pain and suffering allegations and with twenty days leave to amend as to the punitive damages and attorney's fees allegations. Plaintiff's allegations regarding emotional distress and pain and suffering fail because she does not allege any facts showing that she experienced a physical injury nor is it likely that this defect can be cured by an amendment. Plaintiff has leave to allege: (1) ultimate facts supporting punitive damages per Civil Code 3294 and 3340 and (2) a common law, statutory, or contractual basis for fees.
Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976
